seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with Pydantic AI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with Pydantic AI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with Pydantic AI enables type-safe, structured email processing workflows including intent detection, draft generation, and approval flows. Guardrails are essential due to the high-risk nature of email handling, where errors can lead to PII leaks, inappropriate tone, or unauthorized sends. Vex integration provides robust verification against these failure modes, ensuring production reliability.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with Pydantic AI
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant follows a modular pipeline: incoming emails trigger an IntentDetector agent that classifies actions (reply, forward, archive). DraftGenerator creates context-aware responses using Pydantic models for structured output. AttachmentHandler extracts and validates files, while ApprovalWorkflow enforces human review for high-risk actions like external sends. All agents leverage Pydantic AI's result validators for type safety.

Agents communicate via dependency injection, with shared context for email metadata and user preferences. Streaming responses enable real-time draft previews, but require mid-generation validation to prevent partial invalid outputs. Tool calls handle external APIs (SMTP, file storage) with retry logic that Vex monitors for persistent failures.

The architecture scales via async processing, with Vex checkpoints at each agent's result_validator to catch validator rejections, tool masking, and context loss.

risks.name: PII Leakage in Drafts
risks.description: Agent generates responses containing unfiltered personal data from email context
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filtering verifier scans all outputs before approval workflow

risks.name: Inappropriate Tone Detection Failure
risks.description: Sarcastic or aggressive language slips through validator due to model variance
risks.mitigation: Vex tone analysis checkpoint with customizable sentiment thresholds

risks.name: Invalid Recipient Expansion
risks.description: Agent suggests incorrect email addresses during CC/BCC generation
risks.mitigation: Vex recipient validation against corporate directory before send

risks.name: Confidentiality Breach
risks.description: Internal-only content forwarded externally without approval
risks.mitigation: Vex confidentiality classifier blocks sensitive content in external flows

implementation.title: Intent Detection Agent
implementation.description: Core agent that classifies email intent using Pydantic models with Vex result validator

implementation.title: Draft Generation with Structured Output
implementation.description: Generates email drafts with Pydantic validation and streaming support
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import StreamingAgent
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class EmailDraft(BaseModel):
    subject: str = Field(..., max_length=100)
    body: str = Field(..., max_length=5000)
    recipients: list[str]
    attachments: list[str] = []
    tone: str  # 'professional', 'friendly', 'urgent'

async def draft_generator(email_context: str, intent: EmailIntent):
    agent = StreamingAgent(
        model='gpt-4o',
        result_model=EmailDraft,
        result_validator=VexResultValidator(),
        system_prompt=f"""
        Generate professional email draft for intent: {intent.action}
        Context: {email_context}
        Ensure tone: {intent.priority}
        """
    )
    
    async for chunk in agent.run_stream(email_context):
        if chunk.is_complete:
            yield chunk.data

# Usage
intent = EmailIntent(action='reply', priority='high', confidence=0.92)
draft_stream = draft_generator(raw_email, intent)

implementation.title: Approval Workflow Orchestrator
implementation.description: Final decision gate with human-in-loop for high-risk emails
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from vex import Vex
from enum import Enum

class SendDecision(str, Enum):
    APPROVE = 'approve'
    REJECT = 'reject'
    ESCALATE = 'escalate'

class ApprovalContext(BaseModel):
    draft: EmailDraft
    risk_score: float
    requires_human_review: bool
    suggested_action: SendDecision

async def approval_orchestrator(draft: EmailDraft, intent: EmailIntent):
    vex = Vex(client_id='your-key')
    
    # Risk assessment
    risk_result = await vex.verify({
        'draft': draft.model_dump(),
        'intent': intent.model_dump(),
        'verifiers': ['confidentiality', 'recipient_validation', 'tone_appropriateness']
    })
    
    agent = Agent(
        model='gpt-4o',
        result_model=ApprovalContext,
        system_prompt="Conservative approval - escalate uncertain cases"
    )
    
    context = ApprovalContext(
        draft=draft,
        risk_score=risk_result.score,
        requires_human_review=risk_result.score > 0.7,
        suggested_action=SendDecision.APPROVE if risk_result.passed else SendDecision.ESCALATE
    )
    
    return await agent.run(context.model_dump())

vexIntegration.title: Vex Result Validator Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Custom Pydantic AI result validator that integrates Vex verification
vexIntegration.code:
from pydantic_ai import ResultValidator
from vex import Vex, Verifier
from pydantic import BaseModel

class VexResultValidator(ResultValidator):
    def __init__(self, verifiers: list[str] = None):
        self.vex = Vex(client_id='vex-your-key')
        self.verifiers = verifiers or [
            'pii_filter',
            'tone_check',
            'confidentiality'
        ]
    
    async def __call__(self, output: BaseModel) -> bool:
        result = await self.vex.verify({
            'output': output.model_dump(),
            'verifiers': self.verifiers,
            'context': {'use_case': 'email_assistant'}
        })
        return result.passed

# Usage in Pydantic AI agent
agent = Agent(
    result_model=EmailDraft,
    result_validator=VexResultValidator([
        'pii_filter',
        'tone_check',
        'recipient_validation'
    ])
)

vexIntegration.title: Streaming Validation Middleware
vexIntegration.description: Handles mid-stream validation failures common in Pydantic AI streaming
vexIntegration.code:
from pydantic_ai import StreamingAgent
from vex import Vex

class VexStreamingValidator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.vex = Vex(client_id='vex-your-key')
    
    async def validate_stream_chunk(self, chunk: dict, context: dict):
        if 'content' in chunk:
            result = await self.vex.verify({
                'partial_content': chunk['content'],
                'verifiers': ['pii_filter', 'tone_check'],
                'context': context
            })
            return result.passed
        return True

# Integration with streaming agent
async def safe_streaming_draft():
    validator = VexStreamingValidator()
    agent = StreamingAgent(result_model=EmailDraft)
    
    async for chunk in agent.run_stream(email_context):
        if not await validator.validate_stream_chunk(chunk, {'intent': 'reply'}):
            raise ValueError('Stream validation failed')
        yield chunk

vexIntegration.title: Tool Call Monitoring Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Prevents tool retry logic from masking persistent Vex failures
vexIntegration.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, tool
from vex import Vex

vex_client = Vex(client_id='vex-your-key')

@tool
def send_email(draft: dict):
    """Send email with Vex pre-flight check"""
    # Vex verification before SMTP call
    result = vex_client.verify({
        'email_draft': draft,
        'verifiers': ['final_pii_scan', 'confidentiality', 'recipient_validation'],
        'max_retries': 1  # Fail fast on persistent errors
    })
    
    if not result.passed:
        raise ValueError(f'Vex blocked send: {result.issues}')
    
    # Actual SMTP send
    return {'status': 'sent', 'message_id': 'abc123'}

# Agent with monitored tools
agent = Agent(
    model='gpt-4o',
    tools=[send_email],
    result_validator=VexResultValidator()
)

# Vex monitors tool retry patterns
result = await agent.run({'draft': email_draft})
